meaning: adv.漂浮着,随波逐流地adj.漂泊的
examples: The survivors were adrift in a lifeboat for six days.
In 2011, they released a landmark study titled "Academically Adrift," which documented the lack of intellectual growth experienced by many people enrolled in college.
Our plans had gone badly adrift.
And without a why, an organization is effectively adrift and cannot sustain any form of greatness.
Esher lie in 13th place in National One, two places and 14 points adrift of safety.
